name: Amplitude
tagline: Product analytics for understanding user behavior
description: Behavioral analytics with cohorts, funnels, and experimentation
pros: Track every user action without SQL
Built-in A/B testing with significance engine
Cohort analysis by behavior, not demographics
cons: Enterprise plans exceed $50K/year for high volume
Less mature for web analytics vs Google Analytics

content.title: Executive Summary
content.body: Behavioral analytics with cohorts, funnels, and experimentation Amplitude earns a 4.5/5 rating from 3,200 user reviews.

content.title: TL;DR
content.body: Amplitude is a leading analytics platform rated 4.5/5 across 3,200 reviews. Pricing starts at Free–custom enterprise. Best for Product Managers and Data Teams.

content.title: Rating Overview
content.body: Amplitude scores 4.5/5 overall with features (4.6/5), ease of use (4.2/5), support (4.1/5), value (3.8/5), performance (4.4/5).

content.title: Quick Verdict
content.body: Amplitude earns a 4.5/5 rating recommended for Product Managers and Data Teams. Delivers strong feature depth with functional design.

content.title: Company Background
content.body: Amplitude was founded in 2012 in San Francisco, USA, serving 2,000+ customers with 1,000+ employees.

content.title: Product Overview
content.body: Amplitude is a product analytics for understanding user behavior. Provides 18 features across Core, Experimentation, AI, Analytics, Integrations, Mobile, Operations, Security categories. Self-hosted available. Mobile on iOS and Android. API available.

content.title: Key Features
content.body: Essential analytics features:
items: Behavioral Analytics: Event-based user action tracking.
Web SDK: JavaScript with SPA support.

content.title: Feature Deep Dive
content.body: Behavioral Analytics. Event-based user action tracking. Funnel Analysis. Step-by-step conversion drop-off. Cohort Analysis. Behavioral segment comparisons.

content.title: AI Features
content.body: Amplitude AI includes AI anomaly detection, Behavioral cohorts, Predictive analytics.

content.title: API & Automation
content.body: Amplitude provides a comprehensive API for custom integration and workflow automation.

content.title: Security
content.body: Amplitude holds SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA certifications with TLS 1.2+ and AES-256 encryption.

content.title: Compliance
content.body: Amplitude complies with GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, SOC 2 regulations.

content.title: Integrations
content.body: Integrates with Segment, Snowflake, Slack, Jira, Mixpanel, Google Analytics, Salesforce, Zapier, and 2+ more.

content.title: Pricing
content.body: Amplitude uses freemium with usage-based scaling. Free tier available. Plans from Free–custom enterprise.

content.title: Pros
content.body: Amplitude advantages:
items: Track every user action without SQL
Built-in A/B testing with significance engine

content.title: Cons
content.body: Considerations:
items: Enterprise plans exceed $50K/year for high volume
Less mature for web analytics vs Google Analytics

content.title: Performance
content.body: Strong performance with fast load times.

content.title: Ease of Use
content.body: Moderate learning curve with comprehensive documentation.

content.title: User Experience
content.body: Functional organized interface.

content.title: Customer Support
content.body: Support rated 4.1/5 via email and knowledge base.

content.title: Industry Fit
content.body: Best for Analytics and SaaS with Product Managers and Data Teams teams.

content.title: Team Size Fit
content.body: Amplitude accommodates teams of all sizes with generous free tier. Best ROI for 5-50 person teams.

content.title: Real Use Cases
content.body: A product managers team uses Amplitude to streamline analytics workflows and improve team productivity.

content.title: Competitor Analysis
content.body: Amplitude competes with mixpanel, google-analytics. Primary differentiators: feature depth and pricing.

content.title: Alternatives
content.body: Consider mixpanel, google-analytics based on specific team requirements.

content.title: Migration Guide
content.body: Migration rated moderate complexity. Budget 2-4 weeks for full transition. Parallel testing recommended.

content.title: Buying Advice
content.body: Evaluate: feature alignment, total cost at Free–custom enterprise, migration effort (2-4 weeks), and real-data trial.

content.title: Final Verdict
content.body: Amplitude earns 4.5/5. Strong choice for Product Managers and Data Teams. Recommended for teams prioritizing behavioral insights.

faqs.question: What is Amplitude best used for?
faqs.answer: Amplitude is best used for behavioral analytics with cohorts, funnels, and experimentation.

faqs.question: How much does Amplitude cost?
faqs.answer: Amplitude uses freemium with usage-based scaling starting at Free–custom enterprise. Free tier available.

faqs.question: Does Amplitude integrate with other tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, integrates with Segment, Snowflake, Slack, Jira, Mixpanel. And 5+ more.

faqs.question: Is Amplitude suitable for small teams?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a generous free tier. Freemium with usage-based scaling scales with team growth.

faqs.question: How does Amplitude pricing work?
faqs.answer: Amplitude uses freemium with usage-based scaling from Free–custom enterprise, with free tier available.

faqs.question: Is Amplitude secure?
faqs.answer: Yes, holds SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA certifications with encryption.

faqs.question: What integrations does Amplitude offer?
faqs.answer: Integrates with Segment, Snowflake, Slack, Jira, Mixpanel, Google Analytics, and more. API available.

faqs.question: Is Amplitude good for small businesses?
faqs.answer: Yes, with free tier. Freemium with usage-based scaling scales with business size.

faqs.question: What are Amplitude's main advantages?
faqs.answer: Track every user action without SQL and Self-service analytics for product managers.

faqs.question: What are Amplitude's limitations?
faqs.answer: Enterprise plans exceed $50K/year for high volume and Requires engineering for event instrumentation.

faqs.question: How does Amplitude compare to mixpanel?
faqs.answer: Amplitude differentiates through feature depth and performance.

faqs.question: Does Amplitude support team collaboration?
faqs.answer: Yes, includes collaboration features for team workflows.

faqs.question: Can I customize Amplitude?
faqs.answer: Yes, via API, custom fields, and templates.

faqs.question: Is Amplitude easy to set up?
faqs.answer: Setup takes a few days with documentation support.

faqs.question: How often does Amplitude update?
faqs.answer: Updates bi-weekly.

faqs.question: What support does Amplitude provide?
faqs.answer: Support rated 4.1/5. Email and knowledge base.

faqs.question: Does Amplitude offer a free version?
faqs.answer: Yes, with core features.

faqs.question: How does Amplitude handle data privacy?
faqs.answer: Complies with GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, SOC 2 regulations.

faqs.question: What ROI can teams expect?
faqs.answer: Positive ROI typically within 3-6 months from improved analytics workflows.

faqs.question: What platforms does Amplitude support?
faqs.answer: Available on Cloud, Self-hosted. Mobile on iOS and Android.

author: StackPilot Team
learningCurve: Moderate — expect 1-3 days to become productive
whoShouldUse: Amplitude is designed for fast-growing startups that need a scalable analytics platform. Its generous free tier and flexible pricing make it accessible for early-stage companies.
whoShouldNotUse: Amplitude is less ideal for small teams on tight budgets, as its advanced features come at a premium price point. Freelancers may find more cost-effective alternatives.
